title: opencode.jsonc is missing the 'model' field
description: Remediate CONFIG001 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: opencode.jsonc must contain a top-level 'model' field in provider/model format (e.g. 'minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M2.7').
summary: opencode.jsonc must contain a top-level 'model' field in provider/model format (e.g. 'minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M2.7').

title: opencode.jsonc is missing the 'default_agent' field
description: Remediate CONFIG002 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: opencode.jsonc must contain a top-level 'default_agent' field pointing to the team-leader agent (e.g. 'myproject-team-leader').
summary: opencode.jsonc must contain a top-level 'default_agent' field pointing to the team-leader agent (e.g. 'myproject-team-leader').

title: Repeated diagnosis packs are re-reporting the same repair-routed findings without any intervening package or process-version change
description: Remediate CYCLE002 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Stop rerunning subject-repo audit until Scafforge package work changes the managed workflow contract or process version, then rerun one fresh audit against the updated package.
repair_class: Scafforge package work required before the next subject-repo run
summary: Stop rerunning subject-repo audit until Scafforge package work changes the managed workflow contract or process version, then rerun one fresh audit against the updated package.

title: Derived restart surfaces disagree with canonical workflow state, so resume guidance can route work from stale or contradictory facts
description: Remediate WFLOW010 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Regenerate `START-HERE.md`, `.opencode/state/context-snapshot.md`, and `.opencode/state/latest-handoff.md` from canonical manifest, workflow state, and pivot state after every workflow save, compute handoff readiness from bootstrap plus repair-follow-on plus verification state in one shared contract, and fail repair verification if any derived restart surface drifts.
summary: Regenerate `START-HERE.md`, `.opencode/state/context-snapshot.md`, and `.opencode/state/latest-handoff.md` from canonical manifest, workflow state, and pivot state after every workflow save, compute handoff readiness from bootstrap plus repair-follow-on plus verification state in one shared contract, and fail repair verification if any derived restart surface drifts.

title: The generated lease-ownership contract is split across coordinator and worker surfaces, so agents can disagree about who should claim a ticket and when bootstrap gates apply
description: Remediate WFLOW012 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Adopt one lease model everywhere: the team leader owns `ticket_claim` and `ticket_release`, specialists work only inside the already-active ticket lease, and only Wave 0 setup work may claim before bootstrap is ready.
summary: Adopt one lease model everywhere: the team leader owns `ticket_claim` and `ticket_release`, specialists work only inside the already-active ticket lease, and only Wave 0 setup work may claim before bootstrap is ready.

title: The managed smoke-test override contract can fail before the requested smoke command even starts
description: Remediate WFLOW016 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Parse one-item shell-style overrides into argv, treat leading `KEY=VALUE` tokens as environment overrides, and report malformed overrides as configuration errors instead of misclassifying them as runtime environment failures.
summary: Parse one-item shell-style overrides into argv, treat leading `KEY=VALUE` tokens as environment overrides, and report malformed overrides as configuration errors instead of misclassifying them as runtime environment failures.

title: The generated lifecycle contract is not verdict-aware, so FAIL review or QA artifacts can still look advanceable
description: Remediate WFLOW023 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Extract verdicts from the latest review and QA artifacts, route FAIL or BLOCKED outcomes back to implementation, and reject lifecycle transitions when the latest artifact verdict is blocking or unclear.
summary: Extract verdicts from the latest review and QA artifacts, route FAIL or BLOCKED outcomes back to implementation, and reject lifecycle transitions when the latest artifact verdict is blocking or unclear.

title: Fail-state routing is still under-specified in generated prompts or workflow skills
description: Remediate WFLOW024 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Document review, QA, smoke-test, and bootstrap failure recovery paths in ticket-execution, and instruct the team leader to follow transition_guidance.recovery_action whenever it is present.
summary: Document review, QA, smoke-test, and bootstrap failure recovery paths in ticket-execution, and instruct the team leader to follow transition_guidance.recovery_action whenever it is present.

title: Restart-surface tools return paths without verifying what they wrote
description: Remediate WFLOW027 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Return verified flags plus current workflow metadata for published restart surfaces and include size or hash metadata for snapshots so callers can confirm what was written.
summary: Return verified flags plus current workflow metadata for published restart surfaces and include size or hash metadata for snapshots so callers can confirm what was written.

title: opencode.jsonc does not grant 'external_directory' permission
description: Remediate CONFIG003 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Add '"external_directory": "allow"' to the permission block in opencode.jsonc.
summary: Add '"external_directory": "allow"' to the permission block in opencode.jsonc.

title: opencode.jsonc bash permissions missing common commands: ['cp *', 'echo *', 'mkdir *', 'mv *', 'touch *']
description: Remediate CONFIG004 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Add the missing commands to the bash permission allowlist in opencode.jsonc.
summary: Add the missing commands to the bash permission allowlist in opencode.jsonc.

title: The repo-local `ticket-execution` skill is too thin to explain the actual lifecycle contract to weaker models
description: Remediate SKILL002 by correcting the validated issue and rerunning the relevant quality checks.
suggested_fix_approach: Keep `ticket-execution` narrowly procedural: route from `ticket_lookup.transition_guidance`, stop after repeated lifecycle contradictions, reserve `smoke_test` as the only PASS producer, and keep slash commands human-only.
summary: Keep `ticket-execution` narrowly procedural: route from `ticket_lookup.transition_guidance`, stop after repeated lifecycle contradictions, reserve `smoke_test` as the only PASS producer, and keep slash commands human-only.
